D0VKV.content: How to submit the Tankwa Karoo land-use permit
Append research notes to this task

children.name: Summary
children.content: Tankwa land-use is issued by CapeNature. Apply at least 10 working days before arrival, settle the SARS levy, then carry the gate-access permit PDF.

children.name: Txt
children.content: Open the CapeNature land-use application portal and select the Tankwa Karoo regional office.

children.name: Txt
children.content: Attach the AfrikaBurn 2026 event authorisation letter plus the land-use motivation form.

children.name: Txt
children.content: Pay the environmental levy via SARS eFiling and retain the payment reference (PRN).

children.name: Txt
children.content: Submit at least 10 working days before the target date to clear regional review.

children.name: Txt
children.content: On approval, download the gate-access permit and record the permit number on this task.

children.name: Body
children.content: Couldn’t find reliable steps. Try rephrasing or narrowing the request.

cfDjb.content: This is taking a while. Keep it running in the background?
Can’t reach the network. Check your connection and retry.
Allow microphone access to use voice commands.

children.name: Type/Lead
children.content: A leading paragraph that introduces a section with a slightly larger, calmer voice than body copy.

children.name: Type/Body
children.content: Body copy is set in DM Sans for comfortable reading. It wraps within its column and carries the long-form legal and manual prose throughout the app.

children.name: Body
children.content: Delete your account or clear history anytime from Settings. Voice recordings are transcribed, then discarded within 30 days unless you choose to keep them.

children.name: GNote
children.content: New components found by auditing the product boards.

children.name: L
children.content: Both boards predate the library — ZERO ref instances. Every row, pill, tag, status box and rule is ad-hoc inline geometry.

children.name: L
children.content: MAP TO EXISTING:  rows → TaskCard   ·   pills → WindowStatePill   ·   category tags → CategoryTag   ·   range / section / count labels → Label/Mono   ·   header rules → Divider   ·   status box → StatusCycleButton

children.name: L
children.content: NEW (gaps, registered below):  TimelineWeekHeader · TimelineBucket · DependencyConnector · DependencyTreeNode · UnlinkedGroupHeader

children.name: L
children.content: FOLDED (NOT separate components):  NoCliffBucket = TimelineBucket with UNSCHEDULED state   ·   CriticalPathAccent = DependencyConnector with fill:$primary

children.name: Desc
children.content: Week-section header for the Timeline view. ISO-week date range + days-until pill (THIS WEEK / IN 3D / WINDOW CLOSED) + trailing rule. Recurs 6x across the timeline — currently ad-hoc.

children.name: Desc
children.content: ↳ connector glyph that prefixes a dependent (depth>0) node in the dependency tree. Neutral by default; the critical-path highlight is the same component with fill:$primary (see CriticalPathAccent, folded).

children.name: Desc
children.content: Section break for tasks with no dependency edges. unlink icon + UNLINKED mono label + count suffix. Distinct from the DEPENDENCY TREE label; recurs as the tree's terminal group header.

children.name: Desc
children.content: Week-section container: a TimelineWeekHeader followed by a vertical stack of TaskCard instances. Recurs per ISO week (6x). NoCliffBucket is this same component with the header in its UNSCHEDULED / NO CLIFF state — not a separate component.

children.name: Desc
children.content: One indented node in the dependency tree: a DependencyConnector (↳) + a TaskCard instance. Root nodes (depth 0) drop the connector; deeper nodes add 36px indent spacers. Critical-path nodes recolor the connector to $primary.
